Home / Legal / Legal Help in Arkansas
Legal · Arkansas

Legal help in Arkansas.

Custody, child support, and legal aid are run at the state level — here's how to reach Arkansas's official resources.

The rules for custody, child support, and legal aid are set and run by Arkansas, not by any national office. So the most useful thing we can do is send you straight to the official Arkansas doors. These trusted federal and nonprofit directories route you to the right place in your state:

Child Support

Arkansas child support agency

Open a case, make or receive payments, or get help with an existing order through Arkansas's official child support agency.

  • Arkansas child support →
  • Find a local office (federal locator) →
Legal Aid

Free & low-cost legal help in Arkansas

Find nonprofit legal aid you may qualify for, plus self-help resources, through these national finders.

  • LawHelp.org Arkansas →
  • LSC legal aid finder →

New to all this? Start with the plain-English basics that apply everywhere, then use the Arkansas links above for the specifics: Custody & Visitation, Child Support, and Finding Legal Help.

Looking for a different state? Pick your state on the Legal page.
